overallInterpretation: The demo shows a simplified agile workflow with an 8-status flow where tickets start in inRefinement, exit criteria that block status transitions when required fields are missing, a validate-demo command that generates a self-contained context-free prompt for an external validation agent, and a ticket accept command that enforces interactive terminal usage. All four claimed capabilities are exercised through terminal command output.

artifacts.interpretation: Demonstrates the 8-status flow starting from inRefinement. A newly created ticket (task-ESE-0014) lands in inRefinement. Transitioning to readyForDev is blocked by exit criteria listing six missing fields (Description, Acceptance Criteria, Testing Scenarios, Size, Stakeholder Understanding, Demo Deliverable). Attempting to skip a status (inRefinement to inDevelopment) is also rejected. A ticket listing shows multiple tickets in various statuses confirming the flow is active across the project.

Demonstrates the validate-demo command by running it against task-ESE-0006. The output is a complete, context-free prompt containing the demo-expected.json, demo-readme.json, the full text of each artifact, and instructions for a validation agent to produce demo-actual.json. This confirms the command generates a self-contained validation prompt that requires no prior knowledge of the codebase.

Demonstrates that the ticket accept command (run against task-ESE-0010) refuses to proceed when not in an interactive terminal, returning the error 'ticket accept requires an interactive terminal.' This confirms the interactive confirmation gate is enforced.
